content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE

CITY ATTORNEY
OF

LONG BEACH

JOHN R. CALHOUN
CITY ATTORNEY

City Hall
333 West Ocean Boulevard
Long Beach, California 90802
(310) 590-2200

ROBERT E. SHANNON
ASSISTANT

WORKERS' COMPENSATION SECTION
(310) 590-2245

January 14, 1992

Telecopier
(310) 436-1579

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Staff Attorney
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Pursuant to yesterday's telephone conversation we are
requesting the written advice of your office confirming a
telephone conversation with Margaret Ellison on or about
January 3, 1992, concerning a document proposed by Long
Beach Councilmember Tom Clark, who is an elected City
official, to be prepared and mailed to more than two hundred
addressees at public expense. A photocopy of the layout and
draft of the contents of the proposed document is transmitted herewith for your information and use.

During the discussion with Ms. Ellison the proposed
document and its contents were described and read to her,
and based upon that information, Ms. Ellison indicated that
the document was not a prohibited mass mailing under Section
18901(a).

The proposed document does not contain Councilman
Clark's photograph or signature. The only mention of his
name appears (1) in the return address on the outside foldover portion of the document which serves as the "envelope"
part of the document; and (2) as the addressee printed on
the detachable "post-card" type section of the document
which the recipients would fill out and mail back to the
Councilman's office at their own expense if they wish to be
sent information on any of the subjects indicated in that
detachable portion of the document.

There is no specific mention of Councilman Clark's
office in any part of the document. The words "Fourth
District Report" are printed across the bottom of the
outside "envelope" portion of the fold-over document to

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Staff Attorney
California Political Practices Commission
January 14, 1992
Page 2


indicate the nature of the document and the scope of its
distribution. Also, on an inside page, an article regarding
the results of a previous survey of Fourth District residents
is entitled "Fourth District Survey Results." This heading
informs the reader of the source of the survey responses which are reported in the article. Also, on that page is a map of the revised Fourth City Council district which resulted from a recent City Council action to reapportion and redraw the nine City Council District lines. The document contains a legend beside the map that it depicts the Fourth District. This map legend does not refer to Councilman Clark's office, but informs the reader what the map portrays.

The other information in the document consists of several short articles concerning various City activities and an announcement of a "clean sweep" program for the Fourth District which involves the pick up of unusual and oversized items of rubbish that normally are not allowed to be collected as part of the regular City's weekly household refuse service.

Ms. Ellison indicated that an elected official's name and address in the return address on the outside "envelope" portion of a fold-over document, and the inclusion of an elected official's name and address on the detachable "postcard type" request for additional information did not constitute a prohibited mass mailing. Ms. Ellison also stated that in her opinion the references to the "Fourth District" as set forth in the proposed document did not constitute a mention of the Councilman's office, and would not constitute a prohibited mass mailing under 18901(a)(2).

Additionally, the document does not in any way advocate the election of Councilman Clark or refer to his candidacy and therefore, would not be a prohibited mailing under Section 85300.

Because of the necessity to print and distribute this document in adequate time for the residents to be appropriately informed regarding the clean sweep refuse collection project, it is respectfully requested that your response to us be received by January 28, 1992. If you have any questions or desire any additional information, please

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Staff Attorney
California Political Practices Commission
January 14, 1992
Page 3


do not hesitate to contact me. Your cooperation and prompt
attention to our request for advice and assistance is
greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,


[Signature]
JOHN R. CALHOUN
CITY ATTORNEY

JRC:f1
Enclosure
cc: Dr. Tom Clark
    Councilman, Fourth District

VIA FACSIMILE AND U.S. MAIL
